response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `reverse_words` function using pytest:

```python
import pytest

def reverse_words(s: str) -> str:
    return ' '.join(s.split()[::-1])


class TestReverseWords:
    """Test cases for the reverse_words function."""
    
    # Happy path tests
    def test_reverse_words_basic(self):
        """Test basic word reversal."""
        assert reverse_words("hello world") == "world hello"
    
    def test_reverse_words_multiple_words(self):
        """Test reversing multiple words."""
        assert reverse_words("the quick brown fox") == "fox brown quick the"
    
    def test_reverse_words_three_words(self):
        """Test reversing exactly three words."""
        assert reverse_words("one two three") == "three two one"
    
    # Edge cases
    def test_reverse_words_single_word(self):
        """Test with a single word."""
        assert reverse_words("hello") == "hello"
    
    def test_reverse_words_empty_string(self):
        """Test with an empty string."""
        assert reverse_words("") == ""
    
    def test_reverse_words_only_whitespace(self):
        """Test with only whitespace characters."""
        assert reverse_words("   ") == ""
        assert reverse_words("\t\n  ") == ""
    
    def test_reverse_words_leading_trailing_spaces(self):
        """Test with leading and trailing spaces."""
        assert reverse_words("  hello world  ") == "world hello"
        assert reverse_words("   hello   world   ") == "world hello"
    
    def test_reverse_words_multiple_spaces_between(self):
        """Test with multiple spaces between words."""
        assert reverse_words("hello    world") == "world hello"
        assert reverse_words("one  two   three") == "three two one"
    
    def test_reverse_words_mixed_whitespace(self):
        """Test with mixed whitespace characters (tabs, newlines)."""
        assert reverse_words("hello\tworld") == "world hello"
        assert reverse_words("hello\nworld") == "world hello"
        assert reverse_words("hello \t\n world") == "world hello"
    
    def test_reverse_words_special_characters(self):
        """Test with words containing special characters."""
        assert reverse_words("hello! world?") == "world? hello!"
        assert reverse_words("test@email.com another-word") == "another-word test@email.com"
    
    def test_reverse_words_numbers(self):
        """Test with numeric strings."""
        assert reverse_words("123 456 789") == "789 456 123"
        assert reverse_words("1.5 2.7") == "2.7 1.5"
    
    def test_reverse_words_mixed_alphanumeric(self):
        """Test with mixed alphanumeric words."""
        assert reverse_words("word1 word2 word3") == "word3 word2 word1"
        assert reverse_words("test123 abc456") == "abc456 test123"
    
    def test_reverse_words_unicode_characters(self):
        """Test with unicode characters."""
        assert reverse_words("café résumé") == "résumé café"
        assert reverse_words("hello 世界") == "世界 hello"
    
    def test_reverse_words_very_long_string(self):
        """Test with a very long string."""
        words = [f"word{i}" for i in range(1000)]
        input_str = " ".join(words)
        expected = " ".join(reversed(words))
        assert reverse_words(input_str) == expected
    
    # Error cases
    def test_reverse_words_none_input(self):
        """Test that function raises TypeError for None input."""
        with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
            reverse_words(None)
    
    def test_reverse_words_non_string_input(self):
        """
